name: Ireland
description: The Emerald Isle — Dublin, Guinness, Joyce, and a lot of green.

questions.q: What is the capital of the Republic of Ireland?

source.notes: On the east coast at the mouth of the River Liffey.

questions.q: What is Ireland's famous green-hued nickname?

source.notes: Named for its famously lush, green countryside.

questions.q: Which three-leafed plant is Ireland's national symbol?

source.notes: St. Patrick used it to explain the Trinity, per legend.

questions.q: What famous Irish holiday is celebrated each March 17 worldwide?

source.notes: Saint of Ireland's patron; parades worldwide.

questions.q: Which iconic dark dry stout originated in Dublin in 1759?

source.notes: Brewed at St. James's Gate Brewery.

questions.q: Ireland's currency today is which unit?

source.notes: Ireland adopted the euro in 1999/2002.

questions.q: What is the name of Ireland's largest island-surrounding body of water on the west side?

source.notes: Ireland's west coast faces the Atlantic; Irish Sea separates it from Great Britain to the east.

questions.q: Which catastrophe in the 1840s reduced Ireland's population by roughly a quarter through starvation and emigration?

source.notes: Roughly 1 million died; another million emigrated.

questions.q: What is the name of the Irish-language prime-minister-equivalent head of government?

source.notes: Tánaiste is the deputy.

questions.q: What is the name of the Irish-language legislature (parliament)?

source.notes: The Oireachtas comprises the Dáil (lower house) and Seanad (senate).

questions.q: The 1916 armed rebellion against British rule in Dublin is known as what?

source.notes: Began Easter Monday, April 24, 1916.

questions.q: Which 1998 accord largely ended the three-decade conflict in Northern Ireland?

source.notes: Signed April 10, 1998, on Good Friday.

questions.q: Which river flows through Dublin?

source.notes: The Liffey famously divides Dublin into 'northside' and 'southside.'

questions.q: The capital of Northern Ireland is which city?

source.notes: Shipbuilding city; Titanic was built there.

questions.q: Which Irish writer is best known for the 1922 Dublin-set modernist novel 'Ulysses'?

source.notes: June 16 is celebrated as 'Bloomsday' in the book's honor.

questions.q: Which cliffs on Ireland's west coast are one of the country's most visited natural attractions?

source.notes: County Clare, rising over 700 ft above the Atlantic.

questions.q: The longest river in Ireland is which?

source.notes: Runs 224 miles, draining much of central Ireland.

questions.q: Which political party that shaped the 20th-century Republic was founded by Éamon de Valera?

source.notes: Founded 1926.

questions.q: In which year did the Irish Free State become the Republic of Ireland?

source.notes: Republic of Ireland Act came into force April 18, 1949.

questions.q: Ireland's most famous mountain range, a long arc running through the north and west, is the what?

source.notes: In County Kerry, home to Carrauntoohil, Ireland's highest peak.

questions.q: Which Irish rock band formed in Dublin in 1976 and has released albums including 'The Joshua Tree'?

source.notes: Fronted by Bono.

questions.q: Ireland joined the European Union (then European Economic Community) in which year?

source.notes: Joined January 1, 1973, along with the UK and Denmark.
